signals.title: USD/AUD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Fed Ideology Shift
signals.text: USD outperforms as the G8 leader (+0.670%) while AUD lags (-0.934%), creating a massive 1.605% spread. The divergence is accelerated by headlines regarding new Fed Chair Kevin Warsh portending a significant shift in ideology, coinciding with a bear steepener in the US yield curve (2s10s +59.7bp). Despite a -60bp OIS carry disadvantage for USD, the policy shift narrative and rising US 10Y yields (4.667%) dominate price action. AUD/USD at 0.7115 remains under pressure as the weakest G8 performer.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry + Options Skew + Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 159.04 remains supported by a +280bp SOFR-TONA carry advantage, yet the options market signals acute caution. 25d Risk Reversals show a strong -1.52 put skew, indicating institutional demand for JPY protection despite the carry bid. This divergence between spot carry and options pricing reflects mounting intervention risk as price tests the 160.00 threshold. JPY COT LF net short -70,605 confirms a crowded carry trade vulnerable to a sharp unwind if the BoJ signals a June hike.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs Retail Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -19,437 (specs fading the currency) vs retail sentiment at 84% long (extreme retail long). This institutional vs retail divergence at 0.5839 creates a high-risk environment for retail longs if the RBNZ fails to deliver on hike expectations at the May 27 meeting. Speculative positioning is already near the lower end of the range (22.3% long), suggesting limited further selling capacity unless the RBNZ shifts dovish.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Volatility Risk Premium + Positioning
signals.text: AUD/USD IV at 20.4% vs HV30 at 9.2% generates a massive +11.2% Vol Risk Premium, signaling the options market is pricing in extreme tail risk compared to recent realized moves. This elevated hedging demand coincides with a crowded COT LF net long of +55,794 (74.8% long). With AUD as the G8 laggard (-0.934%), the combination of high vol-premium and long positioning suggests a high probability of a forced liquidation event if 0.7100 fails to hold.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Volatility Premium + ECB Neutrality
signals.text: EUR/USD IV at 7.1% vs HV30 at 5.1% shows a +2.0% VRP, indicating a moderate premium for protection as ECB nominee Moulin signals it is 'too soon' to commit to June action. EUR/USD at 1.1593 is structurally weighed down by a +160bp SOFR-€STR carry disadvantage. Speculative positioning remains neutral (48% long), suggesting the current move is driven by USD strength rather than EUR-specific liquidation. Watch for the ECB meeting on 11 June to redefine the vol regime.
